ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese cabbage soup with minced pork; mapped to the broader 'soup food product' class as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Vietnamese cabbage and minced pork soup; A0CKP (Soups and broths) is the closest defensible category-level anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for this Vietnamese soup dish; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Vietnamese cabbage soup with minced pork is approximately 400 g, typically served as a side soup alongside steamed rice in a Vietnamese home-style meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Soup, vegetable beef, canned, condensed (SR Legacy) and Pork, ground, raw (Foundation Foods), used as component analogy for Vietnamese cabbage and minced pork soup
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Vietnamese cabbage soup with minced pork exists in HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated by component analysis: cabbage (USDA FDC #169975), ground pork (USDA FDC #168352), fish sauce (USDA FDC #173580), and water, weighted to reflect a typical Vietnamese canh preparation (approximately 60% broth/water, 20% cabbage, 18% minced pork, 2% seasoning). Input per_100g values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this component estimate and found to be broadly consistent; values retained with medium confidence. Sodium of 237.5 mg/100g is plausible given fish sauce seasoning.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on vegetable soup with meat (GI ~32)
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese cabbage soup with minced pork. GI estimated at 32 based on the closest analogous entry in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables: vegetable soup with meat (GI ~30–35, low category). Carbohydrate content is very low at 2.25 g/100g, predominantly from cabbage (low-GI vegetable). Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (32 × 9.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 2.9, rounded to 1.6 per 100g basis for consistency; GL per serving = (32 × 2.25 × 4) / 100 = 2.9 g per 400 g bowl.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 237.5 mg per 100 g, primarily from fish sauce; a 400 g bowl delivers approximately 950 mg sodium (~41% of the 2300 mg daily limit).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced fish sauce or substitute with low-sodium fish sauce, and avoid adding extra seasoning, to keep the bowl's sodium contribution below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate at 2.25 g per 100 g with negligible sugar (1.0 g/100g) and a low estimated GI of 32, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 2.9 per 400 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This soup is an excellent low-glycaemic-load option for people with Type 2 Diabetes and can be paired with a small portion of low-GI rice or noodles to form a balanced meal without causing significant postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at 1.13 g per 100 g and trans fat is negligible at 0.03 g per 100 g; however, sodium at 237.5 mg per 100 g (950 mg per bowl) is a meaningful cardiovascular risk factor.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, this soup is acceptable in moderation with sodium reduction strategies applied (low-sodium fish sauce, reduced seasoning volume), and the lean protein from minced pork supports a heart-healthy dietary pattern when consumed in appropriate portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minced pork is a red meat high in arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid), which amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute minced pork with minced chicken, fish, or tofu to reduce arachidonic acid load while retaining the anti-inflammatory benefits of cabbage (sulforaphane, vitamin C) and garlic (polyphenols).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canh bắp cải thịt bằm (Vietnamese Cabbage Soup with Minced Pork) is a traditional Vietnamese clear broth soup prepared by simmering tender shredded cabbage and seasoned minced pork in a light stock flavoured with fish sauce, garlic, spring onion, and black pepper. The soup is characteristically light, savoury, and mildly sweet from the cabbage, with a clean broth that is neither creamy nor thickened. It is a staple side dish in Vietnamese home cooking, typically served alongside steamed jasmine rice as part of a multi-dish family meal. Nutritionally, the soup is low in calories (55 kcal per 100 g), very low in carbohydrates (2.25 g per 100 g), and provides moderate protein (4.5 g per 100 g) from the minced pork. Sodium is moderate at 237.5 mg per 100 g due to fish sauce seasoning, making sodium management the primary dietary consideration. The estimated glycemic index is low (~32), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Cabbage contributes dietary fibre (0.75 g per 100 g), vitamin C (7.5 mg per 100 g), and sulforaphane, a glucosinolate with anti-inflammatory properties. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to pork and fish sauce content. For rheumatoid arthritis patients, substituting pork with fish or tofu is recommended to reduce arachidonic acid intake.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this dish was found in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values were estimated by component analysis using USDA FoodData Central entries for cabbage, ground pork, and fish sauce, and cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The component-based estimate is consistent with the input values, supporting medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed international GI tables.
notes: 1. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese cabbage and minced pork soup preparation are not available as a composite dish entry in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases as of April 2026. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g (1.2 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central ground pork fatty acid profile (FDC #168352) weighted to the pork fraction of the dish; these are approximate. 3. potassium_mg (220.0 mg) is estimated from cabbage and pork component contributions per USDA FDC data; not directly measured for this dish. 4. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero; all are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for this dish type. 5. Halal status is marked false as standard pork-containing dishes are not halal; consumers should verify preparation method if halal certification is required. 6. The input source listed FAO/INFOODS as supporting 'menu_listing' only; nutrient values from the input were not treated as FAO/INFOODS-sourced data and were independently cross-validated via USDA FDC component analysis.
